rationale: Probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/record/bundle.py, all KILLED. Clauses: (1) the verification data is in the bundle: writing it under a different name (`verification_path = out_dir / "verification.yml"` -> `"verification-data.yml"`) KILLED ((out/'verification.yml').is_file() plus the manifest files set); (2) the traceability matrix is RENDERED from that data: rendering with an empty context (`context = {"verification": ...}` -> `{}`) KILLED ('total=1 verified=1', 'row:DI-1:verified', and no '{%' template markers may remain); (3) the faithfulness verdicts travel with the bundle: `shutil.copy2(verdict, verdicts_out / verdict.name)` -> `pass` KILLED (verdict file present + manifest files set); (4) a manifest describing the bundle: emptying its files list (the rglob condition `if p.is_file() and p.name != "manifest.json"` -> `if False`) KILLED by the exact files-set equality, with on-disk manifest == returned manifest and the design_inputs/verified/faithfulness_verdicts counts asserted; (5) written to an output directory for retention: every assertion reads back from the out/ directory.
